Off Calendar Nov-30-2016 Continued To Feb-27-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In Alternative Summary Adjudication Of Issues Against Defendant Ruseliz Luna
Off Calendar Dec-08-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. In re: PLAINTIFF JAVIER GARCIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In Alternative Summary Adjudication Of Issues Against Defendant Ruseliz Luna -
Plaintiff Javier Garcia's motion for summary judgment against defendant Ruseliz Luna is denied and his alternative motion for summary adjudication of Ms. Luna's second, third, fourth and eighth affirmative defenses is granted as to the second, fourth and eighth affirmative defenses and denied as to the third affirmative defense. Mr. Garcia's motion for summary judgment is denied for two reasons. First, he has not presented any evidence of the amount of his damages, which is a required element of his sole cause of action for motor vehicle negligence and summary judgment does not lie as to a portion of a cause of action. Second, there is a triable issue whether Mr. Garcia was contributorily negligent based on the deposition testimony of Ms. Luna that she had a green light when she entered the intersection where her vehicle collided with Mr. Garcia's motorcycle. Ms. Luna no longer wishes to pursue her second affirmative defense for statute of limitations and her eighth affirmative defense for failure to use a seatbelt, so Mr. Garcia is entitled to summary adjudication on those defenses. Mr. Garcia satisfied his burden of production that Ms. Luna has no evidence to prove her fourth affirmative defense of comparative negligence of third parties and Ms. Luna has not presented any evidence creating a triable issue on that defense. Ms. Luna has presented sufficient evidence - her deposition testimony that she had a green light when she entered the intersection where her vehicle collided with Mr. Garcia's motorcycle (Luna Depo. 25:24-26:11) - creating a triable issue on her third affirmative defense of comparative negligence of Mr. Garcia, precluding summary adjudication on that defense. Mr. Garcia's characterization of the cited portions of Ms. Luna's deposition as speculation and conjecture lacks merit. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
